name: Citizens
description_short: Citizens is a traditional bank offering checking, savings, credit cards, home loans, and student loans through physical branches and mobile banking.
description_long:
Citizens Bank operates as a full-service retail bank with physical branch locations across major metropolitan areas, including the Long Island City, New York location profiled here. The company has been providing consumer and commercial banking services, positioning itself as a mainstream financial institution serving individual and business customers. Citizens offers a comprehensive suite of banking products designed to serve customers across different life stages and financial needs, from basic checking accounts to complex lending products.

The bank's service offerings include checking accounts with low minimum balances, savings accounts ranging from basic savings to money market accounts and CDs, home borrowing solutions covering down payments and closing costs, student loan products with college savings planning, and credit cards advertised with low APR and rewards benefits. They also provide online and mobile banking access through the Citizens Mobile App, which allows customers to check balances, pay bills, transfer money, and locate ATMs and branches. The Long Island City branch is open Monday-Friday 9 AM-5 PM with Saturday-Sunday closure and can be reached at (718) 289-2506.

Citizens distinguishes itself through a traditional branch-based banking model with physical locations serving as community banking centers where customers can speak with bankers about mortgages, education financing, and retirement planning. The bank emphasizes the ability to discuss financial questions directly with staff rather than purely digital interactions. Their mobile app is positioned as free and secure, offering convenience for customers who prefer digital access while maintaining the option for in-person banking relationships.

As a traditional bank, Citizens operates with standard banking hours (typically 9 AM-5 PM weekdays) which may not suit customers requiring extended access. The company is subject to standard banking regulations and FDIC insurance limitations. Customers seeking 24/7 customer service or no-fee accounts with premium digital-only features may find limitations compared to neobanks, though Citizens provides stability and comprehensive product offerings typical of established financial institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Multiple full-service branch locations in the Long Island City and Queens area for in-person banking
Free mobile banking app with features for balance checking, bill payment, money transfers, and ATM/branch location
Diverse product offerings including checking, savings, CDs, mortgages, student loans, and credit cards in one institution
Low minimum balance requirements advertised for checking accounts
Ability to speak with bank staff about complex financial decisions like home purchases, college funding, and retirement
Weekend hours available (Saturday 9 AM-1 PM) at some locations for customer convenience
Online account enrollment and appointment scheduling available
cons: Limited weekday hours (9 AM-5 PM) and closed weekends at the Queens Plaza location, restricting access for working customers
No drive-up service at the Long Island City location, requiring in-person visits for some services
Standard banking fees likely apply (not detailed on this location page), which may be higher than neobank alternatives
Website content focuses on branch services with limited detail about specific account features, fee schedules, or product terms
No mention of competitive rates for savings products or mortgages compared to online-first lenders
best_for: Customers seeking comprehensive banking in one place with access to mortgages, student loans, and credit products
Individuals who value in-person relationships with bankers for major financial decisions
People in the Long Island City, Queens, Brooklyn, and Woodside areas wanting nearby physical branch access
Customers who want both mobile banking convenience and the option for face-to-face financial advice
diagnosis: Citizens Bank is best for consumers in the Long Island City/Queens area who want traditional branch banking with comprehensive products and the ability to discuss major financial decisions face-to-face with bankers. The main caveat is limited weekday-only hours (9 AM-5 PM) and no drive-up service at this location, which may inconvenience working customers or those seeking convenience banking.
services: Checking accounts with low minimum balances
Savings accounts (basic, money market, and CDs)
Student loans and college savings planning
Credit cards with low APR and rewards
